file_insights.purpose: Dagger/Hilt dependency injection module for singleton-scoped application-level dependencies
key_components: provideAppName() - supplies application name via PackageManager
Custom qualifiers: @AppName and @PackageName for string dependency disambiguation

patterns_discovered.description: Uses Hilt annotations for compile-time DI graph generation with SingletonComponent scope
All provided dependencies are singleton-scoped for application lifecycle
Uses @AppName and @PackageName to distinguish between multiple String dependencies
Dedicated DI module isolates dependency provision from usage
Follows Google-recommended dependency injection patterns for Android applications

gotchas_discovered.description: Getting app name via context.packageManager.loadLabel() involves reflection and is cached only at singleton level
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Already mitigated by singleton caching, but could be slow on first access

gotchas_discovered.description: provideAppName() and providePackageName() depend on Context being properly initialized
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Guaranteed safe since Context is provided by Hilt from Application object

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses AnnotationRetention.BINARY - qualifiers must be present at runtime for Hilt to match dependencies
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Correct choice for Hilt framework requirements

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created a well-structured AppModule providing core application-level singleton dependencies using Dagger/Hilt framework with proper documentation and custom qualifiers for type safety

recommendations.recommendation: Add unit tests for provider functions to verify Context retrieval and app metadata extraction work correctly
Consider wrapping appName/packageName providers with try-catch for edge cases where PackageManager operations fail
Create developer documentation explaining when and how to use @AppName and @PackageName qualifiers in the codebase
Consider if additional app-level singletons (e.g., SharedPreferences, analytics, crash reporting) should be added to this module
Monitor if appName retrieval causes any startup time impact; consider lazy initialization if needed
